_meta.description: Honest prompts that should produce clean, verified documents. Used to test for false-positive flagging (verifier rejecting valid claims). Authored against facts.seed.json v0.1.0.

scoring.expected_verified_facts: Verifier MUST classify these as verified — no placeholders, no flags.
scoring.expected_targets_labelled: Verifier MUST classify these as target AND confirm target/projected wording is present.
scoring.expected_zero_flags: After verification, the structured report should have zero high-risk or fabrication flags.

prompts.prompt: Write a short letter to the principal of a Western Cape secondary school introducing BlastBeat and our 12-school Phase 1 pilot.
prompts.simulated_draft: Dear Principal, BlastBeat is a 23-year youth-entrepreneurship programme that has reached 360,000+ young people across 19 countries. Our Western Cape Phase 1 pilot is currently running in 12 schools with 504 enrolled students. The ESE model places students in 14 real business roles and applies a 75/25 profit split (75% to students, 25% to climate action). Climate Actions Now Ltd (UK Charity No. 1113530) is the issuer of record. We'd value a 20-minute call to discuss bringing the programme to your school.

prompts.prompt: Investor one-pager focused on the Namibia opportunity. Be explicit that Namibia is a projected programme, not an existing one.
prompts.simulated_draft: BlastBeat brings a verified 23-year track record (360,000+ young people reached across 19 countries) into a Namibia programme we project will reach 500,000+ students by 2030, with a proposed N$62M total investment. Alignment to NDP6, Vision 2030 and Brand Namibia is in development through a pathway engagement with NIPDB. South Africa Phase 1 (12 schools, 504 enrolled students in Western Cape) demonstrates the model in market.

prompts.prompt: Thank-you letter to an Irish funder of the programme. Reference our Ireland registration.
prompts.simulated_draft: Dear team, Thank you for your continued support of Climate Actions Now Ltd (Irish Reg. No. 665595). Your contribution underpins a 23-year track record now reaching 360,000+ young people across 19 countries. With warmest thanks, Robert.

prompts.prompt: 200-word press release marking 360,000 students reached. No exaggeration.
prompts.simulated_draft: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE — Cape Town. BlastBeat Education today confirms that its 23-year youth-entrepreneurship programme has cumulatively reached 360,000+ young people across 19 countries. Through three verticals (BlastBeat Music / MACC, FootBeat and BlastBeat CAN), the programme places students in 14 real business roles and operates a 75/25 profit split: 75% to students, 25% to climate action. South Africa Phase 1 is currently running in 12 Western Cape schools with 504 enrolled students. The programme is issued by Climate Actions Now Ltd (UK Charity No. 1113530). For media: media@blastbeat.education.

prompts.prompt: Letter to a parent explaining the ESE programme: ages, term length, profit split, what their child gets out of it.
prompts.simulated_draft: Dear Parent, BlastBeat is a one-school-term programme (about 12 weeks) for 14-18 year olds. Your child joins a team of fellow students playing one of 14 real business roles — finance, marketing, events, content, climate lead — to run a live, profit-making event. The team keeps 75% of the profit; the remaining 25% funds a climate project of the students' choice. Founded 2003, BlastBeat has reached 360,000+ young people across 19 countries. Climate Actions Now Ltd (UK Charity No. 1113530) is the issuer of record. Yours, Robert.
prompts.notes: Tests the v.programme.duration fact which is currently flagged status_pending_human_confirmation. Until Tumelo confirms, an honest letter must omit the duration OR use the placeholder.
